destinations.evidence: TxScheduled contains gas, fee, target, value, input, origin_tx, call_id, index, initial_block, frequency, ttl, caller, and async/SPC linkage fields.
destinations.snippet: pub struct TxScheduled { pub gas: u64, pub max_fee_per_gas: U256, pub max_priority_fee_per_gas: U256, pub to: Option<Address>, pub value: U256, pub input: Bytes, pub origin_tx: B256, pub call_id: u64, pub index: u64, pub initial_block: u64, pub frequency: u64, pub ttl: u64, pub caller: Address, ... }

destinations.evidence: Port synthesis reads originHash from Scheduler storage and constructs TxScheduled with call id, index, initial block, frequency, ttl, caller, fees, value, and execute calldata.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in port plus alloy: event-state tracking is replaced by parent-state synthesis, but generated scheduled transactions preserve the required origin and recurrence metadata.
rationale: The current scheduler code tracks origin_hash from the event transaction hash, requires enhanced event fields when tracking calls, and creates TxScheduled values from the caller-supplied origin_hash/execution_index and stored call metadata.
statement: Tracked CallScheduled events and generated scheduled transactions must preserve the registration transaction hash, call id, execution index, start_block/initial_block, frequency, num_calls, ttl, gas and fee fields, value, caller, and calldata needed to execute the scheduler call deterministically.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate Scheduler storage with non-default recurrence/origin/caller/fee/value data and assert synthesized TxScheduled preserves it.
summary: Generated scheduled transactions retain origin and recurrence metadata.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduled obligation synthesis should round-trip Scheduler metadata into TxScheduled fields.
title: Scheduler calls retain origin and recurrence metadata
